summary.intent: Address Codecov's 98.5% patch coverage complaint on PR #636 by handling uncovered branches in crates/oauth/src/redirect.rs, then fully resolve all open PR review comments including a missed Greptile P2 about observability.
summary.outcome: Eliminated two provably-unreachable defensive branches to satisfy Codecov, added tracing::debug! instrumentation when the redirect URI rewrite fires to address the Greptile P2, committed and pushed both changes, and resolved all open review threads.

repo: The oauth crate uses tracing for observability; tracing was already a dependency before this session.
normalize_loaded_redirect_uri was extracted in commit 2c6174e2 as a prior refactor that incidentally auto-resolved a Greptile thread without explicitly addressing the substance.
The project runs tests across moltis-oauth, moltis-mcp, and moltis-provider-setup (240 tests total).

code.finding: host() on a parsed https URL is always Some because https is a WHATWG special scheme — the None arm was dead code. Replaced match with is_some_and() to move the None case into stdlib.

set_scheme() only fails when switching between special and non-special schemes; https→http is special→special so the error branch was unreachable. Changed to let _ = parsed.set_scheme("http") with a comment.
Added tracing::debug! call when redirect URI rewrite fires, per Greptile P2 recommendation for production observability.

workflow: Greptile review threads can appear auto-resolved when a refactor touches the relevant line, even if the underlying concern wasn't addressed — worth reviewing thread substance, not just resolution status.
CodSpeed benchmark regressions/improvements on unrelated benchmarks (e.g. env_substitution) can appear on PRs without being caused by the PR changes.
Eliminating unreachable branches is preferable to writing tests for them when the unreachability can be proven from type/scheme invariants.

friction: A Greptile P2 comment was missed initially because the thread was auto-resolved by a prior refactor touching the same line, requiring a second pass over all review threads.
